texto: When the artist is alive in any person, whatever his kind of work may be, he becomes an inventive, searching, daring, self-expressive creature. He becomes interesting to other people. texto: Lines are results, do not draw them for themselves. ... Lines give birth to lines. Drawing is not following a line on the model, it is drawing your sense of the thing. ... texto: I have no sympathy with the belief that art is the restricted province of those who paint, sculpt, make music and verse. I hope we will come to an understanding that the material used is only incidental, that there is artist in every man; and that to him the possibility of development and of expression and the happiness of creation is as much a right and as much a duty to himself, as to any of those who work in the especially ticketed ways.

texto: In every human being there is the artist, and whatever his activity, he has an equal chance with any to express the result of his growth and his contact with life. I don't believe any real artist cares whether what he does is 'art' or not. Who, after all, knows what art is?

frase_original: You pass people on the street, some are for you, some are not.

texto: The work of the art student is no light matter. Few have the courage and stamina to see it through. You have to make up your mind to be alone in many ways. We like sympathy and we like to be in company. It is easier than going it alone. But alone one gets acquainted with himself, grows up and on, not stopping with the crowd. It costs to do this. If you succeed somewhat you may have to pay for it as well as enjoy it all your life.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Robert Henri: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de enfrentar incertezas com ação. O trecho “The work of the art student is no light matter. Few have the courage and stamina to see it throug…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, art, alone pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e reflexao.

texto: Good composition is like a suspension bridge - each line adds strength and takes none away.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Robert Henri: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Good composition is like a suspension bridge - each line adds strength and takes none away.” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, good, bridge pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bridge e line.

texto: Because we are saturated with life, because we are human, our strongest motive is life, humanity; and the stronger the motive back of the line the stronger, and therefore more beautiful, the line will be.

texto: Be game--take a chance--don't hide behind veils and veils of discretion... Go forward with what you have to say, expressing things as you see them. You are new evidence, fresh and young. Your work, the spirit of youth, you are the progress of human evolution. If age dulls you it will be time enough then to be ponderous and heavy--or quit. It takes a tremendous amount of courage to be young, to continue growing--not to settle and accept.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Robert Henri sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “Be game--take a chance--don't hide behind veils and veils of discretion... Go forward with what y…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a fe, age, art, útil em momentos de termino e trabalho.
